sourceMovie.title: Swingers
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Swingers works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Entourage
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Swingers clicked for you, Entourage (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Swingers?
faq.answer: Try Entourage, Withnail & I, Broadway Danny Rose—each shares DNA with Swingers while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Swingers?
faq.answer: Swingers (1996) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Swingers worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
